steps.title: Identify what counts as a finding
steps.content: A study’s finding is its key result or outcome — not its setup, methodology, or background. In the notes, the finding is the 65.3% statistic: the quantified result showing which logo version participants selected.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Evaluate each answer choice against that standard:
  • Choice A — When participants were asked to identify the correct version of the Bic logo, 65.3% selected the unaltered image.: This presents the core quantitative result of the study with just enough task context to make it meaningful. ✓
  • Choice B — Participants were asked to identify the correct version of the Bic logo, which contains a cartoon man holding a pen.: This describes the task and logo content but omits the actual outcome — the percentage result. ✗
  • Choice C — The Bic logo contains a cartoon man holding a pen, but in one of the altered versions, the man was holding a pencil.: This describes the stimulus materials used in the study, not the finding itself. ✗
  • Choice D — In a 2021 study, researchers asked participants to identify the correct version of the Bic logo.: This is purely methodological background with no outcome stated. ✗
That matches Choice A — When participants were asked to identify the correct version of the Bic logo, 65.3% selected the unaltered image..
